mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical difference between above-trend and below-trend GDP quarters (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly increases in the federal funds rate elevate discount rates and signal aggressive tightening, causing immediate equity valuation compression and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decline significantly following months where the funds rate jumps exceed 0.25pp (Rank IC > -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and stable unemployment rates confirm a healthy labor market without overheating, supporting consumer spending and corporate margins to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns fail to outperform when PAYEMS exceeds 150k and UNRATE remains below 4.5% (median return difference < 0.5%).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, which historically precedes equity drawdowns by compressing forward earnings expectations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of quarters following a 5% peak-to-trough decline in housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate revenue growth and reduces inflationary pressures, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with month-over-month industrial production growth (Pearson r < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers fears of persistent stagflation and delayed central bank easing, forcing equity risk premiums higher and depressing S&P 500 valuations within one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns do not decline significantly when monthly CPI growth exceeds 0.4% (t-stat > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized and core inflation below 2.0% signals a soft-landing environment that optimizes the discount rate and earnings outlook, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns fail to exceed baseline when GDP growth > 2.5% and CPI < 2.0% (median outperformance < 0.3%).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points acts as a leading indicator of recessionary labor market deterioration, causing investors to price in earnings contractions and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns do not turn negative when the three-month unemployment rate change exceeds 0.5pp (median return > 0%).
